skill.name: astropy
skill.description: Use Astropy for astronomy and astrophysics workflows involving coordinates, units, FITS files, cosmology, time systems, tables, WCS, and related data analysis.
skill.summary: Astronomy data analysis guidance for Astropy coordinates, units, FITS, cosmology, time, tables, and WCS.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high-severity alerts, but review found they are mostly Markdown and astronomy terminology false positives. The skill is publishable with a medium warning because it documents package installation, local FITS/table file access, remote data downloads, and SAMP connections.

medium_findings.title: Legitimate File and Remote Data Access Requires User Control
medium_findings.description: The skill documents FITS file reads, update and append modes, memory mapping, remote FITS access, downloads, and SAMP connections. These are expected Astropy workflows but should only run on user-approved files and URLs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly show file I/O, remote access, downloads, and SAMP connection examples. They are legitimate scientific examples, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Package Installation Commands Are Documentation Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill includes shell commands to install Astropy and optional dependencies. They are not hidden execution logic, but assistants should not run them without user consent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible installation guidance in a bash code fence. There is no evidence of dynamic command construction or automatic execution.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Ruby Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Most external command alerts occur on Markdown code fences or inline module names. They are documentation formatting and Python examples, not Ruby shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown fences, inline references, or Python snippets. No Ruby code or shell interpolation pattern is present in context.

low_findings.title: Astropy SAMP Misclassified as Windows SAM Database Access
low_findings.description: The critical sensitive findings point to Astropy SAMP, Simple Application Messaging Protocol. This is astronomy interoperability documentation, not Windows credential database access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The section heading expands SAMP and the code imports astropy.samp. There is no reference to Windows SAM files or credential extraction.

low_findings.title: Scientific Terms Misclassified as C2, Weak Crypto, or Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Several blocker alerts are caused by astronomy variables, units, sorting terms, display calls, and file inspection examples. No evidence found of command-and-control, cryptography, or host reconnaissance intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed contexts are normal Astropy examples and best practices. The suspicious labels come from token matches such as variable names, decibels, and inspection methods.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Import Finding Is a Multiline Python Import False Positive
low_findings.description: The scripts alert points to a parenthesized Python import from astropy.visualization. No dynamic import expression or runtime module loading was found at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited code is a static from-import statement split across lines. It does not use Python importlib, JavaScript import(), or user-controlled module names.

content.value_statement: Astronomy workflows often combine coordinates, units, FITS files, and time systems. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through practical Astropy analysis tasks.
actual_capabilities: Guides coordinate creation, frame conversion, angular separation, and catalog matching with Astropy.
Explains unit-aware calculations, unit conversion, equivalencies, and quantity performance patterns.
Covers FITS image and table reading, header access, HDU handling, and memory mapping.
Supports cosmology calculations for distances, ages, lookback times, and built-in cosmological models.
Describes time formats, time scales, TimeDelta arithmetic, and observer-related timing calculations.
Documents WCS pixel-to-world transforms, Astropy tables, visualization helpers, constants, and modeling modules.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not the Astropy package itself.
It does not validate scientific assumptions or calibrate raw observations automatically.
Remote downloads and SAMP workflows require user-approved network access.
Large FITS and catalog operations may need local memory and performance tuning.

use_cases.title: Convert Observing Coordinates
use_cases.description: Transform targets between ICRS, Galactic, FK5, and AltAz frames with clear unit handling.

use_cases.title: Inspect FITS Observations
use_cases.description: Open FITS files, inspect HDUs and headers, and extract image or table data safely.

use_cases.title: Prepare Cosmology Calculations
use_cases.description: Compute luminosity distance, angular diameter distance, age, and lookback time for selected redshifts.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Coordinate Conversion
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner task for a single target position.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Astropy to convert this RA and Dec from ICRS to Galactic coordinates: [coordinates]. Include units and explain each result.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect a FITS File
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate task for local observation data.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me inspect [file name]. Show how to list HDUs, read the science data, and summarize important header keywords.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Match Two Catalogs
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an Astropy workflow to cross-match [catalog A] and [catalog B] using RA and Dec columns with a [separation] tolerance.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Multi-Step Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan an Astropy pipeline for [science goal] using units, coordinates, FITS I/O, WCS, tables, and reproducible validation checks.

output_examples.input: Convert a target from ICRS to Galactic coordinates.
output_examples.output: A concise explanation of the SkyCoord setup, the transformed longitude and latitude, and the units used.

output_examples.input: Inspect a FITS image and summarize its metadata.
output_examples.output: A readable summary of HDU structure, selected header values, data shape, and safe file handling notes.

output_examples.input: Calculate cosmological distances for redshift values.
output_examples.output: A table-style explanation of luminosity distance, angular diameter distance, universe age, and lookback time.

best_practices: State coordinate frames, time scales, and units before doing calculations.
Use context managers for FITS files and verify HDU structure before reading data.
Ask for user approval before installing packages, downloading data, or connecting to SAMP services.
anti_patterns: Do not mix unitless arrays with physical quantities in scientific calculations.
Do not overwrite FITS files unless the user explicitly requested that output.
Do not assume a default cosmology when the research context requires a specific model.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps AI assistants use Astropy for astronomy data analysis, including coordinates, units, FITS, cosmology, time, tables, and WCS.

faq.question: Does it install Astropy automatically?
faq.answer: No. It shows installation commands, but installation should only happen after user approval.

faq.question: Can it work with FITS files?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents common FITS workflows such as opening files, reading HDUs, inspecting headers, and using memory mapping.

faq.question: Does it support remote astronomy data?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for remote FITS access and downloads, which require approved network access.

faq.question: Can it help with coordinate matching?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers SkyCoord, frame transformations, angular separation, and catalog matching workflows.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium warning for legitimate file, network, and installation-command examples.
